size.description: Number of items in each page
size.name: size

public.description:
If set to false (the default value), a long, obfuscated URL
is returned. Its expiration might be sooner than for other upload
targets.

If set to true, a shorter URL is returned and
its expiration is the same as for the other upload targets.

image_types.description: Map of image type names to their details

hyper_v_generation.description:
Choose the VM Image HyperV generation, different features on Azure are available
depending on the HyperV generation.

image_name.description:
Name of the uploaded image. It must be unique in the given resource group.
If name is omitted from the request, a random one based on a UUID is
generated.

location.description:
Location of the provided resource_group, where the image should be uploaded and registered.
How to list all locations:
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/account?view=azure-cli-latest#az_account_list_locations'
If the location is not specified, it is deducted from the provided resource_group.

resource_group.description: Name of the resource group where the image should be uploaded.

subscription_id.description: ID of subscription where the image should be uploaded.

tenant_id.description:
ID of the tenant where the image should be uploaded.
How to find it in the Azure Portal:
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/fundamentals/active-directory-how-to-find-tenant

containers.description: Container images to embed into the final artfact

distro.description:
The distribution to use for the compose. If left empty the host
distro will be used.

modules.description: An alias for packages, retained for backwards compatability

directories.description: Directories to create in the final artifact

files.description: Files to create in the final artifact

filesystem.description: List of filesystem mountpoints to create

installation_device.description: Name of the installation device, currently only useful for the edge-simplified-installer type

partitioning_mode.description:
Select how the disk image will be partitioned. 'auto-lvm' will use raw unless
there are one or more mountpoints in which case it will use LVM. 'lvm' always
uses LVM, even when there are no extra mountpoints. 'raw' uses raw partitions
even when there are one or more mountpoints.

repositories.description:
Repositories to write to /etc/yum.repos.d/ in the final image. Note
that these are not used at build time.

BlueprintFile.description: A custom file to create in the final artifact.

data.description: Contents of the file as plain text

group.description: Group of the file as a gid or a group name

mode.description: Permissions string for the file in octal format

user.description: Owner of the file as a uid or a user name

ports.description: List of ports (or port ranges) and protocols to open

policy_id.description:
Puts a specified policy ID in the RHSM facts, so that any instances registered to
insights will be automatically connected to the compliance policy in the console.

module_hotfixes.description: Disables modularity filtering for this repository.

gid.description: Group id to use instead of the default

groups.description: A list of additional groups to add the user to

password.description:
If the password starts with $6$, $5$, or $2b$ it will be stored as
an encrypted password. Otherwise it will be treated as a plain text
password.

uid.description: User id to use instead of the default

name.description: The name of the subvolume, which defines the location (path) on the root volume

part_type.description: The partition type GUID for GPT partitions. For DOS partitions, this field can be used to set the (2 hex digit) partition type. If not set, the type will be automatically set based on the mountpoint or the payload type.

blueprint_id.description:
Optional blueprint ID to record in RHSM facts. This is set automatically
when composing from a blueprint via image-builder.

items.description: The SBOM documents for each image built in the compose.

The SBOM documents for the given image. Each image usually has
at least two SBOMs, one for the build environment and one for the
actual content of the image.

name.description: Name to use for the container from the image

source.description: Reference to the container to embed

name.description: Name for the created container image

tag.description: Tag for the created container image

digest.description: Digest of the manifest of the uploaded container on the registry

custom_repositories.description:
Extra repositories for packages specified in customizations. These
repositories will be used to depsolve and retrieve packages. Additionally,
these packages will be saved and imported to the `/etc/yum.repos.d/` directory
on the image

installation_device.description: Name of the installation device, currently only useful for the edge-simplified-installer type

partitioning_mode.description:
Select how the disk image will be partitioned. 'auto-lvm' will use raw unless
there are one or more mountpoints in which case it will use LVM. 'lvm' always
uses LVM, even when there are no extra mountpoints. 'raw' uses raw partitions
even when there are one or more mountpoints.

payload_repositories.description:
Extra repositories for packages specified in customizations. These
repositories will only be used to depsolve and retrieve packages
for the OS itself (they will not be available for the build root or
any other part of the build process). The package_sets field for these
repositories is ignored.

set_releasever.description: Set the releasever DNF variable, tying the system to a specific release of RHEL

Directory.description: A custom directory to create in the final artifact.

ensure_parents.description: Ensure that the parent directories exist

group.description: Group of the directory as a group name or a gid

mode.description: Permissions string for the directory in octal format

user.description: Owner of the directory as a user name or a uid

allOf.description: Complete metadata for a specific distribution from images library

architectures.description: Map of architecture names to their details

module_platform_id.description: Module platform ID for DNF modularity

os_version.description: Full OS version including minor version

releasever.description: Release version used in repo files

additionalProperties.description: Map of architectures to their repositories.

DistributionList.description: Map of distributions to their architecture.

File.description: A custom file to create in the final artifact.

data.description: Contents of the file as plain text

ensure_parents.description: Ensure that the parent directories exist

group.description: Group of the file as a gid or a group name

mode.description: Permissions string for the file in octal format

user.description: Owner of the file as a uid or a user name

min_size.description: size of the filesystem in bytes

fs_type.description: The filesystem type. Swap partitions must have an empty mountpoint.

part_type.description: The partition type GUID for GPT partitions. For DOS partitions, this field can be used to set the (2 hex digit) partition type. If not set, the type will be automatically set based on the mountpoint or the payload type.

ports.description: List of ports (or port ranges) and protocols to open

FirewallServices.description: Firewalld services to enable or disable

FirewallZones.description:
Bind a list of network sources to a zone to restrict traffic from
those sources based on the settings of the zone.

name.description:
name of the zone, if left empty the sources will apply to
the default zone.

sources.description: List of sources for the zone

bucket.description: Name of an existing STANDARD Storage class Bucket.

image_name.description:
The name to use for the imported and shared Compute Engine image.
The image name must be unique within the GCP project, which is used
for the OS image upload and import. If not specified a random
'composer-api-<uuid>' string is used as the image name.

region.description:
The GCP region where the OS image will be imported to and shared from.
The value must be a valid GCP location. See https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/locations.
If not specified, the multi-region location closest to the source
(source Storage Bucket location) is chosen automatically.

share_with_accounts.description:
List of valid Google accounts to share the imported Compute Engine image with.
Each string must contain a specifier of the account type. Valid formats are:
  - 'user:{emailid}': An email address that represents a specific
    Google account. For example, 'alice@example.com'.
  - 'serviceAccount:{emailid}': An email address that represents a
    service account. For example, 'my-other-app@appspot.gserviceaccount.com'.
  - 'group:{emailid}': An email address that represents a Google group.
    For example, 'admins@example.com'.
  - 'domain:{domain}': The G Suite domain (primary) that represents all
    the users of that domain. For example, 'google.com' or 'example.com'.
If not specified, the imported Compute Engine image is not shared with any
account.

gid.description: Group id of the group to create (optional)

name.description: Name of the group to create

size.description:
Size of image, in bytes. When set to 0 the image size is a minimum
defined by the image type.

upload_targets.description:
The type and options for multiple upload targets. Each item defines
a separate upload destination with its own options. Multiple
different targets as well as multiple targets of the same kind are
supported.

pipeline_name.description:
The name of the osbuild pipeline which has the packages described
in the SBOM installed.

pipeline_purpose.description:
The purpose of the pipeline. The `buildroot` pipeline was used for
the build environment dueing the image build. The `image` pipeline
represents the actual content of the image. Due to the nature of
some image types, there may be multiple pipelines of the same
purpose.

sbom.description: The SBOM document in the 'sbom_type' format.

sbom_type.description: The type of the SBOM document. Currently only SPDX is supported.

ImageTypeInfo.description: Image type metadata from images library

aliases.description: Alternative names for this image type

exports.description: Names of stages that produce the build output

iso_label.description: ISO label (only for ISO image types)

ostree_ref.description: Default OSTree ref for this image type

payload_package_sets.description: Package set names safe for custom packages via custom repos

required_blueprint_options.description: Customization options required by this image type

supported_blueprint_options.description: Customization options supported by this image type

append.description: Appends arguments to the bootloader kernel command line

name.description: Name of the kernel to use

languages.description: List of locales to be installed, the first one becomes primary, subsequent ones are secondary

fs_type.description: The filesystem type for the logical volume. Swap LVs must have an empty mountpoint.

name.description: Name of the module to enable.

contenturl.description:
A URL which, if set, is used for fetching content. Implies that `url` is set as well,
which will be used for metadata only.

parent.description: Can be either a commit (example: 02604b2da6e954bd34b8b82a835e5a77d2b60ffa), or a branch-like reference (example: rhel/8/x86_64/edge)

rhsm.description:
Determines whether a valid subscription manager (candlepin) identity is required to
access this repository. Consumer certificates will be used as client certificates when
fetching metadata and content.

policy_id.description:
Puts a specified policy ID in the RHSM facts, so that any instances registered to
insights will be automatically connected to the compliance policy in the console.

name.description:
Name of the package to install. File globbing is supported,
eg. 'openssh-*'

version.description:
Optional version of the package to install. If left blank the
latest available version will be used. Wildcards are supported
eg. '4.11.*'

checksum.description: Optional package checksum using ALGO:HASH form

repository.description: Repository to import the ostree commit to

Repository.description:
Repository configuration.
At least one of the 'baseurl', 'mirrorlist', 'metalink' properties must
be specified. If more of them are specified, the order of precedence is
the same as listed above.

check_repo_gpg.description: Enables gpg verification of the repository metadata

module_hotfixes.description: Disables modularity filtering for this repository.

package_sets.description:
Naming package sets for a repository assigns it to a specific part
(pipeline) of the build process.

rhsm.description: Determines whether a valid subscription is required to access this repository.

key.description: Adds the key to the user's authorized_keys file

user.description: User to configure the ssh key for

packages.description:
Array of package names to search for. Supports * wildcards for
names, but not for versions.

disabled.description: List of services to disable by default

enabled.description: List of services to enable by default

masked.description: List of services to mask by default

done.description: Amount of completed steps in the build.

total.description: Total amount of steps in the build.

content_sets.description: Optional list of repository IDs to enable with subscription-manager on first boot.

insights_client_proxy.description: Optional value to set proxy option when registering the system to Insights

patch_url.description: Optional value used to specify the patch host when registering the system with Insights.

rhc.description: Optional flag to use rhc to register the system, which also always enables Insights.

template_name.description: Optional value to register with a template when using rhc to register the system with Insights.

template_uuid.description: Optional value to register with a template when registering the system with Insights.

timezone.description: Name of the timezone, defaults to UTC

UploadOptions.description:
Options for a given upload destination.
This should really be oneOf but AWSS3UploadOptions is a subset of
AWSEC2UploadOptions. This means that all AWSEC2UploadOptions objects
are also valid AWSS3UploadOptionas objects which violates the oneOf
rules. Therefore, we have to use anyOf here but be aware that it isn't
possible to mix and match more schemas together.

password.description:
If the password starts with $6$, $5$, or $2b$ it will be stored as
an encrypted password. Otherwise it will be treated as a plain text
password.

name.description: Volume group name (will be automatically generated if omitted)

part_type.description: The partition type GUID for GPT partitions. For DOS partitions, this field can be used to set the (2 hex digit) partition type. If not set, the type will be automatically set based on the mountpoint or the payload type.

info.description: Service to build and install images.
info.title: OSBuild Composer cloud api

get.description:
Get the status of a running or completed image from a compose.
This includes whether or not the image creation succeeded.

parameters.description: ID of image status to get
parameters.name: id

get.summary: The status of a cloned compose

post.description: Create a new compose, potentially consisting of several images and upload each to their destinations.

get.description:
Get the list of composes. They may be completed, uploaded,
locally saved, or failed.

delete.description: Delete a compose and all of its independent jobs.

get.description:
Get the status of a running or completed compose.
This includes whether or not the compose succeeded.

parameters.description: ID of compose status to get
parameters.name: id

201.description: The new image is being created

get.description: Download the  artifact of a finished compose.

200.description: The metadata for the given compose.

get.summary: Download the artifact for a compose.

get.description: Get the status of a running or finished compose. This includes whether or not it succeeded, and also meta information about the result.

parameters.description: ID of compose status to get
parameters.name: id

200.description: The logs for the given compose, in no particular format (though valid JSON).

get.description: Get the manifests of a running or finished compose. Returns one manifest for each image in the request. Each manifest conforms to the format defined at https://www.osbuild.org/man/osbuild-manifest.5

parameters.description: ID of compose status to get
parameters.name: id

200.description: The manifest for the given compose.

get.summary: Get the manifests for a compose.

get.description:
Get the metadata of a finished compose.
The exact information returned depends on the requested image type.

parameters.description: ID of compose status to get
parameters.name: id

200.description: The metadata for the given compose.

get.summary: Get the metadata for a compose.

get.description:
Returns a set of SBOM documents for each image in the request.
SBOM documents are in the SPDX format.

parameters.description: ID of compose for which to get SBOMs
parameters.name: id

200.description: The SBOMs for the given compose.

get.summary: Get the SBOMs for a compose.
Get all of the supported distribution repository details

parameters.description: Filter by image type. Multiple values can be specified.
parameters.name: image_type

parameters.description: Filter by architecture. Multiple values can be specified.
parameters.name: architecture

get.summary: Get details for a specific distribution
Get a list of all possible errors

get.description: Get an instance of the error specified by id

get.summary: Get the openapi spec in json format

post.summary: Search for detailed information on a list of package names
